summary: Polish composer and violinist (1909–1969), nicknamed 'The First Lady of Polish Music.' Known for her seven violin concertos (which she performed herself until injured in a car accident) and her 1948 Concerto for String Orchestra. Critics labeled her work neoclassical, though she rejected the label. Also wrote a radio opera about King Arthur.

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Polish composer and violinist (1909–1969), nicknamed 'The First Lady of Polish Music.' She performed many of her seven violin concertos herself, until a car accident ended her performing career. Critics labeled her music neoclassical, though she rejected the label. She also wrote a radio opera titled The Adventure of King Arthur. Associated with Polish musical contemporaries Lutosławski, Górecki, and Penderecki. Wikipedia

comprehensive_summary: Grażyna Bacewicz (1909–1969) was a Polish composer and violinist, nicknamed 'The First Lady of Polish Music.' She wrote seven violin concertos, which she performed herself until a car accident ended her performing career. Her most celebrated work is her Concerto for String Orchestra (1948), whose third movement is characteristically marked Vivo. Critics labeled her music neoclassical, though she rejected the label. She is also associated with Polish contemporaries Witold Lutosławski, Henryk Górecki, and Krzysztof Penderecki. Outside her instrumental works, she wrote a radio opera titled The Adventure of King Arthur.

cards.front: Composer: Polish violinist-composer nicknamed 'The First Lady of Polish Music'; wrote and performed seven violin concertos until a car accident ended her performing career
Composer: critics labeled her work neoclassical, though she rejected the label; also wrote a radio opera about King Arthur
